# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { open } from "deno";
const kv = await Deno.openKv(); // Open a KV namespace with default options (if not provided) and get the service instance for it, returns an Async Iterable which yields key/value pairs as they are added or removed in this Namespace 
import { parseBody } from "https://deno.land/x/dave/mod.ts"; // Importing a utility function to handle request body parsing (useful for POST requests) and response formatting, useful when you want your API endpoints return JSON data or other formats like XML 
import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/dave/mod.ts"; // Importing a utility function to handle routing in Deno land's dave module (useful for creating RESTful APIs) and use it as middleware, useful when you want your API endpoints respond with different data types 
import { validate } from "https://deno.land/x/dave/middlewares/validator_mod.ts"; // Importing a utility function to handle request validation (useful for input sanitization) and response formatting, useful when you want your API endpoints return JSON data or other formats like XML 
import { createError } from "https://deno.land/x/dave/middlewares/error_mod.ts"; // Importing a utility function to handle error handling (useful for logging and responding with meaningful errors)  
const router = new Router(); // Create an instance of the dave's routing module 
router.use(validate()); // Use middleware that validates request data, useful when you want your API endpoints return JSON data or other formats like XML (useful for input sanitization and error handling)  
// Define routes here using router methods such as get(), post() etc., use the response object to send responses back 
router.get("/users/:id", async ([params]: [{ id }]) => { // Route handler function, params is an array of URL parameters (useful for querying)  
    const user = await kv.get(["userData", params.id]);// Fetch data from KV store using the dynamically generated key 
     if (!user){ throw createError({ status:404, message:"User not found" }); } // If no such a record is present in our database then return an error with HTTP Status code of 'Not Found'  
    res.json(user);// Send the user data back as JSON response 
});
const app = Deno.listen("https://deno.land/x/dave"); // Listen for incoming requests on a specific port (useful when you want your API to be accessible from outside)  
await router(app);// Use the dave's routing module as middleware, useful if there are multiple APIs in one application
